task_completion.description: Configure Screaming Frog to use the 'Googlebot Smartphone' User-Agent.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog Configuration > User-Agent dialog showing 'Googlebot Smartphone' (or 'Googlebot (Smartphone)') selected from the preset dropdown or entered manually.

task_completion.description: Configure custom extraction for the viewport meta tag.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog Configuration > Custom > Extraction dialog showing an active rule using CSS Path `meta[name="viewport"]` and set to extract the 'content' attribute (or 'Extract HTML Element' if 'content' is specified in the path).

task_completion.description: Crawl at least 50 pages of https://books.toscrape.com/.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screaming Frog main window showing 'https://books.toscrape.com/' in the URL bar, with the 'Internal' tab displaying a list of at least 50 crawled URLs.

task_completion.description: Export the Internal HTML data to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text editor showing the file `mobile_internal_html.csv` located in `~/Documents/SEO/exports/`.

task_completion.description: Export the Custom Extraction data to the specified directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager, terminal, or text editor showing the file `mobile_custom_extraction.csv` located in `~/Documents/SEO/exports/`. If open, it should show extracted viewport data.

task_completion.description: Document the user-agent, total pages crawled, and presence of viewport tags in a text report.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing `mobile_readiness_report.txt` in `~/Documents/SEO/reports/`. The text must explicitly mention the 'Googlebot Smartphone' user-agent, state the total number of pages crawled (>= 50), and note the presence/content of the viewport tags.

integrity.description: The agent used the Screaming Frog SEO Spider GUI to perform the crawl and exports.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the Screaming Frog application interface open and being interacted with, rather than terminal scripts or python scripts mocking the crawl.

integrity.description: The exported CSV files appear to be genuinely generated by Screaming Frog.
integrity.visual_evidence: If the CSV files are visible, they should contain standard Screaming Frog headers (e.g., 'Address', 'Content', 'Status Code' for Internal; 'Address', 'Extraction 1' for Custom Extraction) and not just manually typed minimal data.

integrity.description: The final report contains genuine data derived from the crawl.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report should mention the actual extracted viewport content ('width=device-width') and a specific number of crawled pages, rather than generic placeholders copied from the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target URL: https://books.toscrape.com/. Expected User-Agent: 'Googlebot Smartphone' (or 'Googlebot (Smartphone)'). Expected Custom Extraction CSS Path: `meta[name="viewport"]` extracting the 'content' attribute. The target website actually contains the viewport meta tag with content='width=device-width'. The VLM should look for 'width=device-width' in the custom extraction results and the final report to verify successful extraction.
